canvas.safeZoneLabel: Bottom-right PiP reserved for recording / camera

slides.title: Numbers 0 to 10
speakerNotes: Every number you see in Thailand — prices, floors, phone numbers — starts with these eleven sounds. Learn them now and the whole country opens up.
Count 0-10 in Thai, ask and say phone and room numbers, and say your age using digits in real daily contexts.

textBlocks.heading: Lesson focus
lines: You can understand and say the digits needed for age, room numbers, and basic counting.
Say your age, count objects, and give a simple number clearly.

visualStrategy.onScreenGoal: Introduce the lesson clearly and set the recording tone.
teachingVisuals: Numbers 0 to 10
First Contact and Courtesy
teacherCues: Use the deck title as the recording opener.
Keep the right third clear.
visualStrategy.rationale: The opener should establish identity and objective without visual clutter.

slides.title: What you will learn
speakerNotes: You can understand and say the digits needed for age, room numbers, and basic counting.
You can recycle recent vocabulary while using number pronunciation control in short controlled output.
Say your age, count objects, and give a simple number clearly.
Recognize slowly spoken digits in age and number contexts.

textBlocks.heading: Objectives
lines: You can understand and say the digits needed for age, room numbers, and basic counting.
You can recycle recent vocabulary while using number pronunciation control in short controlled output.
Say your age, count objects, and give a simple number clearly.
Recognize slowly spoken digits in age and number contexts.

visualStrategy.onScreenGoal: Make the lesson goals visible before the teaching slides begin.
teachingVisuals: You can understand and say the digits needed for age, room numbers, and basic counting.
You can recycle recent vocabulary while using number pronunciation control in short controlled output.
Say your age, count objects, and give a simple number clearly.
Recognize slowly spoken digits in age and number contexts.
teacherCues: Pause briefly after each objective.
Keep the slide readable from a distance.
visualStrategy.rationale: Objectives are clearer as cards than as decorative visuals.

slides.title: Zero, one, two
speakerNotes: Let's learn to count in Thai. We will start with just three numbers: zero, one, and two.
Listen first. ศูนย์ sǔun. That means zero. Say it with me: ศูนย์.
Next: หนึ่ง nùeng. That means one. Your turn: หนึ่ง.
And: สอง sǎawng. That means two. Repeat: สอง.
Now let's count together from zero: ศูนย์, หนึ่ง, สอง. Your turn — count from zero to two before I show the answer.

textBlocks.heading: Key language
lines: ศูนย์ | sǔun | 0 — zero
หนึ่ง | nùeng | 1 — one
สอง | sǎawng | 2 — two

textBlocks.heading: Practice
lines: Listen and repeat: ศูนย์, หนึ่ง, สอง. Say each one clearly after me.
Pause-and-produce: count from zero to two in Thai. Go.
Discrimination drill: I say a number. Is it ศูนย์, หนึ่ง, or สอง? Point to the answer.

visualStrategy.onScreenGoal: Show each digit as a large individual triplet card so the learner sees, hears, and reads each number one at a time.
teachingVisuals: Large triplet card for ศูนย์ with numeral 0.
Large triplet card for หนึ่ง with numeral 1.
Large triplet card for สอง with numeral 2.
teacherCues: Introduce each digit one at a time. Pause between each.
Model clearly — the learner should hear each number before reading it.
visualStrategy.rationale: Digits are abstract. Text-only triplet cards teach better than images.

slides.title: Three, four, five
speakerNotes: You already know ศูนย์, หนึ่ง, สอง. Now let's add three more.
Listen first. สาม sǎam. That means three. Say it with me: สาม.
Next: สี่ sìi. That means four. Your turn: สี่. Notice the low tone — your voice stays down.
And: ห้า hâa. That means five. Repeat: ห้า. This one has a falling tone — start high, drop down.
Now count from zero to five: ศูนย์, หนึ่ง, สอง, สาม, สี่, ห้า. Your turn — say the whole sequence before I show it.

textBlocks.heading: Key language
lines: สาม | sǎam | 3 — three
สี่ | sìi | 4 — four
ห้า | hâa | 5 — five

textBlocks.heading: Practice
lines: Listen and repeat: สาม, สี่, ห้า. Say each one after me.
Pause-and-produce: count from zero to five in Thai. Go.
Substitution drill: I say a numeral. You say the Thai word. 3 — your turn. 1 — your turn. 5 — your turn. 0 — your turn.

visualStrategy.onScreenGoal: Show three new digit triplet cards, then a counting sequence 0-5.
teachingVisuals: Large triplet card for สาม with numeral 3.
Large triplet card for สี่ with numeral 4.
Large triplet card for ห้า with numeral 5.
teacherCues: Introduce each digit one at a time. Pause between each.
After all three, run the counting sequence 0-5 as a fluency builder.
visualStrategy.rationale: Digits are abstract. Text-only triplet cards teach better than images.

slides.title: Six, seven, eight
speakerNotes: Good progress. You can count to five. Now let's add หก, เจ็ด, and แปด.
Listen first. หก hòk. That means six. Say it with me: หก.
Next: เจ็ด jèt. That means seven. Your turn: เจ็ด. Notice the short, clipped ending — your tongue hits the roof of your mouth.
And: แปด bpàaet. That means eight. Repeat: แปด. Another clipped ending — the sound stops sharply.
Now count from ศูนย์ to แปด: ศูนย์, หนึ่ง, สอง, สาม, สี่, ห้า, หก, เจ็ด, แปด. Your turn.

textBlocks.heading: Key language
lines: หก | hòk | 6 — six
เจ็ด | jèt | 7 — seven
แปด | bpàaet | 8 — eight

textBlocks.heading: Practice
lines: Listen and repeat: หก, เจ็ด, แปด. Say each one after me.
Pause-and-produce: count from zero to eight. Go.
Discrimination drill: I say a number between zero and eight. Say the English meaning before I show it.

visualStrategy.onScreenGoal: Show three new digit triplet cards for 6, 7, 8.
teachingVisuals: Large triplet card for หก with numeral 6.
Large triplet card for เจ็ด with numeral 7.
Large triplet card for แปด with numeral 8.
teacherCues: Emphasise the clipped stop endings on เจ็ด and แปด.
After teaching all three, run the full 0-8 count.
visualStrategy.rationale: Digits are abstract. Text-only triplet cards teach better than images.

slides.title: Nine, ten — the full count
speakerNotes: Almost there. Just two more numbers and you will have the full set.
Listen first. เก้า gâo. That means nine. Say it with me: เก้า. Falling tone — start high, drop down.
And the last one: สิบ sìp. That means ten. Your turn: สิบ.
Now the big moment. Count from zero to ten: ศูนย์, หนึ่ง, สอง, สาม, สี่, ห้า, หก, เจ็ด, แปด, เก้า, สิบ.
Your turn — count from zero to ten before I show the answer. Take your time.

textBlocks.heading: Key language
lines: เก้า | gâo | 9 — nine
สิบ | sìp | 10 — ten

textBlocks.heading: Practice
lines: Listen and repeat: เก้า, สิบ. Say each one after me.
Pause-and-produce: count from zero to ten in Thai. The full sequence. Go.
Substitution drill: I say a numeral, you say the Thai word. 7 — your turn. 10 — your turn. 4 — your turn. 9 — your turn. 2 — your turn.

visualStrategy.onScreenGoal: Show two new digit triplet cards for 9 and 10, then the full 0-10 sequence.
teachingVisuals: Large triplet card for เก้า with numeral 9.
Large triplet card for สิบ with numeral 10.
Full counting sequence 0-10 in a compact row.
teacherCues: Build excitement — the learner is completing the full set.
After the full count, celebrate the achievement briefly.

slides.title: Numbers in real life
speakerNotes: You can count to ten. Now let's use those digits in real life.
First chunk: เบอร์อะไร ber à-rai. It means what number? You use this to ask for a phone number or any number. Listen first: เบอร์อะไรครับ.
Your turn: เบอร์อะไรครับ or เบอร์อะไรคะ. Good. If someone asks you เบอร์อะไร, you answer digit by digit: ศูนย์ แปด เก้า.
Next chunk: ฉันอายุ...ปี chǎn aa-yú ... bpii. It means I am ... years old. Put any number in the slot. ฉันอายุสิบปี — I am ten years old. ฉันอายุแปดปี — I am eight years old.
Last chunk: ห้อง hâwng. It means room. Add a number after it: ห้องสาม — room three. ห้องเจ็ด — room seven. ห้องห้า — room five.
Let's practise using all three. Someone asks เบอร์อะไรครับ and you answer with digits. Someone asks your age and you say ฉันอายุ...ปี. You need a room and you say ห้อง plus the number.

textBlocks.heading: Key language
lines: เบอร์อะไร | ber à-rai | what number?
ฉันอายุ...ปี | chǎn aa-yú ... bpii | I am ... years old
ห้อง... | hâwng ... | room ...

textBlocks.heading: Practice
lines: Response-building: I ask เบอร์อะไรครับ. Give me a three-digit number using Thai digits. Your turn.
Substitution drill: say your age using ฉันอายุ...ปี. Try with สิบ, then with เก้า, then with ห้า.
Substitution drill: say a room number using ห้อง. Try ห้องสาม, ห้องหก, ห้องสิบ.
Pause-and-produce: I say a room number in English. You say it in Thai. Room 4 — your turn. Room 9 — your turn.

visualStrategy.onScreenGoal: Show three chunk frames with digit slots clearly marked.
teachingVisuals: Chunk frame: เบอร์อะไร with example digits filled in.
Chunk frame: ฉันอายุ [___] ปี with slot for number.
Chunk frame: ห้อง [___] with slot for number.
teacherCues: Model each chunk slowly, then have the learner substitute different digits.
Recycle digits from all four previous sections in the examples.
visualStrategy.rationale: A hotel room door number grounds the abstract digits in a real-world context the learner will encounter.

assets.fallbackReason: No acceptable Openverse or Wikimedia asset could be downloaded automatically.
assets.usageNotes: A hotel room door number grounds the abstract digits in a real-world context the learner will encounter.

slides.title: Pronunciation: tones in Thai numbers
speakerNotes: Let's train your ear. Thai numbers have different tones, and mixing them up can cause confusion.
Listen to these two: สาม sǎam — rising tone, your voice lifts up. Now: ห้า hâa — falling tone, your voice starts high and drops.
Try it. Say สาม with your voice going up. Now say ห้า with your voice going down. Can you feel the difference?
Here is another pair: สอง sǎawng — rising tone. And เก้า gâo — falling tone. Rising goes up, falling goes down.
One more contrast: สี่ sìi has a low tone — your voice stays flat and low. Compare with ห้า hâa falling tone — starts high, drops. Say สี่, then ห้า. Low, then falling.

textBlocks.heading: Key language
lines: สาม | sǎam | rising tone ↗
ห้า | hâa | falling tone ↘
สี่ | sìi | low tone →

textBlocks.heading: Practice
lines: Tone echo: I say สาม. Repeat. Now I say ห้า. Repeat. Can you hear the tone difference?
Minimal pair choice: I say two numbers. Which one has the rising tone — สาม or ห้า? Point before I tell you.

visualStrategy.onScreenGoal: Show tone direction arrows next to number pairs to visualize pitch contrast.
teachingVisuals: Two-column contrast: rising tone numbers (สาม, สอง) vs falling tone numbers (ห้า, เก้า).
Simple pitch arrow: rising arrow for rising, falling arrow for falling, flat arrow for low.
teacherCues: Model each tone slowly and use hand gestures for pitch direction.
Keep this beat short — 60 to 90 seconds.
visualStrategy.rationale: Tone arrows and contrast layout teach better than photographs.

slides.title: Roleplay
speakerNotes: Checking into a hotel — you give your room number, confirm your age, and exchange phone digits with the receptionist.
Staff | สวัสดีค่ะ ห้องอะไรคะ | sà-wàt-dii khâ hâwng à-rai khá | Hello. What room?
You | ห้องห้าครับ | hâwng hâa khráp | Room five.
Staff | อายุเท่าไหร่คะ | aa-yú thâo-rài khá | How old are you?
You | ฉันอายุสามสิบปีครับ | chǎn aa-yú sǎam-sìp bpii khráp | I am thirty years old.
Staff | เบอร์อะไรคะ | ber à-rai khá | What is your number?
You | ศูนย์ แปด เก้า หนึ่ง สอง สาม | sǔun bpàaet gâo nùeng sǎawng sǎam | 0-8-9-1-2-3.
Staff | ขอบคุณค่ะ | khàawp-khun khâ | Thank you.
You | ขอบคุณครับ | khàawp-khun khráp | Thank you.

textBlocks.heading: Dialogue
lines: Staff | สวัสดีค่ะ ห้องอะไรคะ | sà-wàt-dii khâ hâwng à-rai khá | Hello. What room?
You | ห้องห้าครับ | hâwng hâa khráp | Room five.
Staff | อายุเท่าไหร่คะ | aa-yú thâo-rài khá | How old are you?
You | ฉันอายุสามสิบปีครับ | chǎn aa-yú sǎam-sìp bpii khráp | I am thirty years old.
Staff | เบอร์อะไรคะ | ber à-rai khá | What is your number?
You | ศูนย์ แปด เก้า หนึ่ง สอง สาม | sǔun bpàaet gâo nùeng sǎawng sǎam | 0-8-9-1-2-3.

thaiFocus.thai: สวัสดีค่ะ ห้องอะไรคะ
ห้องห้าครับ
อายุเท่าไหร่คะ
ฉันอายุสามสิบปีครับ
เบอร์อะไรคะ
ศูนย์ แปด เก้า หนึ่ง สอง สาม
thaiFocus.translit: sǔun bpàaet gâo nùeng sǎawng sǎam

thaiFocus.thai: ขอบคุณครับ

visualStrategy.onScreenGoal: Support the roleplay recording with clean turn-taking.
teachingVisuals: Checking into a hotel — you give your room number, confirm your age, and exchange phone digits with the receptionist.
teacherCues: Record against the dialogue ladder.
Bottom-right PiP reserved for camera.
visualStrategy.rationale: The dialogue itself is the teaching focus.

slides.title: Recap
speakerNotes: Count 0 to 5 in Thai. Go.
Count 6 to 10 in Thai. Go.
How do you ask 'what number?' in Thai?
Say 'I am ten years old' in Thai.
Say 'room seven' in Thai.

textBlocks.heading: What you can now do
lines: Count 0 to 5 in Thai. Go.
Count 6 to 10 in Thai. Go.
How do you ask 'what number?' in Thai?
Say 'I am ten years old' in Thai.
Say 'room seven' in Thai.

visualStrategy.onScreenGoal: Finish with a simple, memorable checklist.
teachingVisuals: Count 0 to 5 in Thai. Go.
Count 6 to 10 in Thai. Go.
How do you ask 'what number?' in Thai?
Say 'I am ten years old' in Thai.
Say 'room seven' in Thai.
teacherCues: Use this slide as the summary.
Keep the ending calm and uncluttered.
visualStrategy.rationale: A recap slide should be compact and memorable.

slides.title: Closing
speakerNotes: By the end, you can count to ten, say your age, ask for a number, and give a room number in Thai.
Next: First Contact and Courtesy continues.

textBlocks.heading: Takeaway
lines: By the end, you can count to ten, say your age, ask for a number, and give a room number in Thai.
Next: First Contact and Courtesy continues.

visualStrategy.onScreenGoal: Give Nine a clean final slide to close the recording.
teachingVisuals: By the end, you can count to ten, say your age, ask for a number, and give a room number in Thai.
Next: First Contact and Courtesy continues.
teacherCues: Use the closing as the final spoken wrap-up.
visualStrategy.rationale: The closing slide is a presenter anchor, not a new teaching beat.
